_comment: TEMPLATE — copy to mappings/pyarchinit/ and adapt to your table structure. This file lives in the mappings root and is NOT auto-loaded by the MappingRegistry (the registry only scans the pyarchinit/, emdb/, and generic/ subdirectories). It exists as a reference/starting point.
_description: Template for SQLite-based PyArchInit-like tables. Demonstrates: id_column, description column, attribute mapping, filter columns (with the new is_filter / filter_required flags introduced in s3dgraphy 1.6), composite node names via node_name_template (1.6+), and stratigraphic relations.
name: Template — pyArchInit-style SQLite table
description: Replace this description with one specific to your table.

table_settings._comment_format_type: format_type must be 'sqlite' for the PyArchInitImporter.
table_settings._comment_table_name: table_name is the SQLite table to read with SELECT * FROM <table_name>. The importer guards this against a [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* whitelist.
_comment_node_name_template: OPTIONAL (1.6+). When set, the importer composes the human-readable
node name (StratigraphicNode.name) by substituting {column_name}
placeholders with the row's values, instead of using the bare value
of the is_id column.
Syntax: Python str.format-style placeholders. Each placeholder is the
literal name of a column in the source table. Any column may be used
— it does NOT need to be the is_id column or even appear in
column_mappings (though listing it there is recommended).
is_id semantics are UNCHANGED. The is_id column still identifies the
row's stable primary key (and is used by the relations engine to
resolve cross-row edges by id value). node_name_template is a
PRESENTATION-LEVEL concern: it only affects the node's .name string.
NULL / empty handling: components whose value is None or an empty
string are OMITTED from the composite name. Adjacent dots are then
collapsed and leading/trailing dots are stripped. So if
unita_tipo='' and the template is '{area}.{unita_tipo}.{us}' with
area='A' and us='101', the resulting name is 'A.101' (not 'A..101').
If node_name_template is absent (legacy mapping), the importer falls
back to the pre-1.6 behaviour: node_name = str(row[is_id column]).
ENRICH MODE WARNING: in auxiliary-mode imports the importer looks up
existing nodes by name (StratigraphicNode.name), so the labels of
the yEd-curated graph must match the composite naming convention
encoded here. If your existing graph uses bare US numbers as labels
(e.g. '101'), re-importing into it with a composite template ('A.US.101')
will leave every row as an orphan. Either change the template, or
rename the existing labels.

site_name._comment: FILTER COLUMN. A consumer application (CLI, GUI, web form, ...) can call importer.get_filter_columns() to discover this entry, then call importer.get_distinct_values('site_name') to fetch the list of available sites and present a dropdown to the user. Because filter_required=true, the consumer is expected to force the user to pick a value (no 'All values' option). Use this for the natural top-level partition of the table (site, project, season, dataset_id, ...).

area_id._comment: Optional secondary filter, combined with other filters via AND. With filter_required=false the consumer can let the user choose 'All values' to skip this filter. Use for additional partitions inside a site (area, trench, square, ...).

unit_number._comment: is_id=true marks the row identifier. The cell value is the row's stable primary key, used by cross-row relations and (when no node_name_template is set in table_settings) as the StratigraphicNode.name. Exactly ONE column must be marked is_id. node_type selects the StratigraphicNode subclass (US / USV / USD / SF / TSU / ... — see s3dgraphy.utils.utils.get_stratigraphic_node_class).

description._comment: is_description=true: this column's value becomes the StratigraphicNode.description. Optional but recommended for human-readable graphs.

stratigraphic_type._comment: is_attribute=true: this column's value is stored as an attribute on the stratigraphic node itself (not as a separate PropertyNode). property_name overrides the default attribute name. Multiple is_attribute columns are allowed. This column is ALSO referenced by the node_name_template above — that's fine, the template only reads row values, it doesn't care which other role the column plays.

interpretation._comment: Regular property column: creates a PropertyNode for each row, linked to the stratigraphic node via a 'has_property' edge.

relations._comment: Property edge: hooks a PropertyNode column to its parent stratigraphic node. The 'has_property' edge_type is special — it does NOT create cross-row edges, it just links a row's properties to the row's main node.
